receiptStandards.rule: Documents must be actual tax receipts or invoices; booking confirmations will not be sufficient
Online copies of invoices and receipts are sufficient, a hard copy is not required
Receipts and invoices must be submitted alongside expense items
The receipts/invoices should be reported in the local currency

receiptStandards.expenseType: IT equipment, Office supplies, Professional services, Training
receiptStandards.rule: The Local Employer's name and details should appear on invoices: Global PPL CH GmbH

receiptStandards.expenseType: IT equipment, Office supplies, Professional services, Training
receiptStandards.rule: Invoice must include address: Freigutstrasse 2 8002 Zürich, Switzerland

receiptStandards.expenseType: IT equipment, Office supplies, Professional services, Training

receiptStandards.rule: Exception when it is not possible to use the Local Employer's name, e.g. on flight bookings. The workers name should then be used instead
Maximum of CHF 20 for smaller business expenses that are tax-free
No receipts are required since this expense is based on usage rather than bills/invoices/receipt/logbook

compliancePoliciesGrossUpRelated.grossUpRule: All approved expenses will be paid as NET to the employee and grossed up if they are not tax-free
Business expenses related to workers completing their job are usually tax exempt - no gross up required
Only purely business related elements will be tax free, anything additional will be subject to tax and grossed up

compliancePoliciesGrossUpRelated.expenseType: Small business expenses up to CHF 20
compliancePoliciesGrossUpRelated.grossUpRule: Smaller business expenses up to maximum CHF 20 are tax-free - no gross up required
Mileage reimbursed at official rates is tax exempt - no gross up required
Training expenses are tax exempt - no gross up required
Hotel expenses are tax exempt when for business travel - no gross up required
Transportation expenses for business travel are tax exempt - no gross up required

compliancePoliciesAdditionalInfoRelated.additionalInfoRule: Workers should keep all their expenses in a simple report of their choice
Any personal information not required for reimbursement purposes should be removed before it is submitted
The worker will need to add the FX rate they have used to calculate the expense
Workers should submit a separate report per each business trip
A logbook is required for each used car. The worker will need to share a map with the relevant route (google maps is sufficient) including route, car details and destination
If a worker uses more than one vehicle in a year the mileage will all be calculated based on a combined mileage total
Employers can choose not to use per diems and instead reimburse meals against receipts. Cannot mix per diem method with actual expenses on same trip
Per diem can be paid upon the workers return, it does not need to be paid in advance
Training expenses require the approval of the direct manager
Tax exemptions will only be applied providing sufficient proof is shared e.g. tax receipts, invoices etc. Without the correct supporting documents any applicable tax exemption cannot be applied
